title: Meeting Action Item
description: Canonical shape for an action item produced by a meeting facilitator. Used by wisdom:extract_action_items, action-item-store, and the tracking pipeline.

meeting_ref.description: Optional reference back to the source meeting.

title.description: Imperative form, ≤ 120 chars (e.g. 'Send revised proposal to compliance').

summary.description: Optional longer description if the title is not self-explanatory.

kind.description: operator_self triggers self-execution; team_member triggers tracking + reminders; external is observation-only.

person_slug.description: Optional stable slug for the same person across meetings.

channel_handle.description: Optional reminder channel: slack:@user, email:user@org, etc.

due_at.description: Hard deadline. If absent, default reminder cadence is daily.

tenant_slug.description: Optional tenant scope; mirrors mission tenant.

relationship.description: Whether the reminder was sent to the assignee directly (`primary`) or copied to a chain-of-command channel (`cc_manager`). Defaults to `primary` when absent.

execution.description: Populated when assignee.kind = operator_self and Kyberion executes the item.

modality.description: Linguistic modality the action item was extracted from. Only `declarative` is eligible for execute_self / track_pending; others land in `pending_speaker_review`. Default behavior: missing = declarative.

provenance.description: Traceability anchors required for audit (meeting-facilitator outcome simulation, severity high).

review_state.description: Speaker-consent gate. Items extracted from non-declarative modalities default to pending_speaker_review and are NOT eligible for execute_self / track_pending until the speaker confirms. Default behavior: missing = auto_committed.

max_reminders.description: Hard cap on reminder send count for this item; track pipeline stops when reached. Default 5.

policy.description: Governance / policy flags grouped together. Reader migrates legacy flat-form fields (partial_state / restricted / restriction_rule_id / manager_handle at the top level) into this object.

partial_state.description: Ops-3 fail-closed: true when extracted from a meeting whose bridge errored or returned an incomplete listen result. Items in this state are NOT eligible for self-execution or auto-tracking until cleared via clearPartialState.

restricted.description: Compliance-2: true when the item title/summary matches a restricted-action-kinds-policy.json rule. Self-execution is gated on approval pass.

restriction_rule_id.description: Identifier of the rule that matched when `restricted=true`. Used in the audit trail.

manager_handle.description: HR-2: chain-of-command channel handle. Reminder dispatch CCs this when priority is `must` or policy requires manager visibility.
